title: Real executed round count in fleet synthesis + report
description: Plumb the ACTUAL number of rounds executed (which can be less than the configured maxRounds when the early-stop fires) out of FleetCoordinator.executeRounds and into both .bober/fleet-synthesis.json and .bober/fleet-report.json. Closes the Phase-B known limitation where bundle.rounds reported the configured maxRounds cap instead of the rounds that actually ran.

clarificationQuestions.question: On an early-stopped run, what should fleet-synthesis.json's rounds report?

options.label: A
options.description: Rounds executed (the round at which the loop terminated) — natural 'how many rounds ran'.

options.label: B
options.description: Only productive rounds that added new findings (under-reports work done).

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — rounds executed; round 2's children DID run even if they added nothing.

clarificationQuestions.question: How widely should the real round count be surfaced?

options.label: B
options.description: Also in fleet-report.json (PortfolioReport gains an optional rounds field).

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A is smallest; user chose B.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Rounds executed (=2 in the maxRounds=3, stop-after-round-2 example). roundsRun = the round at which the loop terminated; on a full run with no early-stop it equals maxRounds.

Also in fleet-report.json. The real executed count is surfaced in BOTH .bober/fleet-synthesis.json and .bober/fleet-report.json. PortfolioReport gains an OPTIONAL rounds field, present only on blackboard runs so the no-blackboard report stays byte-identical.

Autonomous self-approval — every assumption is backed by cited file:line evidence read this session; the two load-bearing semantic/scope decisions were resolved by the user (Q1=rounds-executed, Q2=also-in-report).

approvalEvidence: src/fleet/coordinator.ts:55-82 (executeRounds returns ChildExecution[]; loop var r holds the terminating round; maxRounds>=1 per manifest schema so the loop always runs >=1)
src/fleet/index.ts:165-213 (runFleet hardcodes roundsRun=maxRounds at :184 with the bober: ceiling comment :181-183; single reporter.build(outcomes) at :199; collect(bb, report, roundsRun) at :205)
src/fleet/reporter.ts:17-62 (PortfolioReport shape + build(outcomes); add optional rounds via guarded second arg)
src/fleet/synthesis.ts:29-39 (collect already takes rounds:number — no change needed)
grep executeRounds: only caller is runFleet (index.ts:185) + the two test files; private coordinator method, not exported — signature change is safe and confined to src/fleet/

assumptions: executeRounds is a private FleetCoordinator method called ONLY by runFleet (index.ts:185) + referenced in coordinator.test.ts/index.test.ts — not exported in the public API, so changing its return shape to { executions, roundsRun } is safe and fully confined to src/fleet/ (verified by grep).

'Rounds executed' = the round at which the loop terminated (the loop variable r at exit). Full run with no early-stop → roundsRun === maxRounds; early-stop after round 2 of a max-3 run → roundsRun === 2. manifest.blackboard.maxRounds is int min(1) max(3) (manifest.ts), so the loop runs >=1 round → roundsRun >= 1.

PortfolioReport.rounds is OPTIONAL and present ONLY on blackboard runs; build(outcomes) called with no rounds (the no-blackboard path) omits the key entirely (JSON.stringify drops undefined), keeping the no-blackboard fleet-report.json byte-identical to today. runFleet passes rounds only when bb is set: reporter.build(outcomes, bb ? { rounds: roundsRun } : undefined).

synthesis.ts collect(blackboard, childResults, rounds) already accepts rounds:number — no change to synthesis.ts; only the VALUE passed (the real roundsRun instead of the maxRounds cap) changes, plus removing the bober: ceiling comment at index.ts:181-183.
The no-blackboard path (coordinator.execute) is untouched — it never produces a rounds count and writes no synthesis file; roundsRun stays 0 and is never surfaced there.
outOfScope: Changing the early-stop logic, the rounds cap (BLACKBOARD_MAX_ROUNDS / manifest maxRounds 1-3), or any Phase-B behavior other than the count value.
Adding rounds to the no-blackboard fleet-report.json (must stay byte-identical) or writing a synthesis file on no-blackboard runs.
Logging the round count to stdout/history, or any new CLI surface/flag.
Any change to the SharedBlackboard module, the config.fleet/manifest schema, or the agent-bober blackboard CLI.
Distinguishing 'productive' rounds (rejected Q1 option B) — the count is rounds executed, not rounds-that-added-findings.

features.title: Plumb real executed round count to synthesis + report
features.description: Return the actual executed round count from executeRounds and thread it into both fleet-synthesis.json (replacing the maxRounds-cap value) and fleet-report.json (a new optional rounds field, blackboard runs only).
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When a blackboard fleet runs all maxRounds rounds (no early-stop), fleet-synthesis.json.rounds === maxRounds and fleet-report.json.rounds === maxRounds.
AC2: When a blackboard fleet early-stops after round 2 of a maxRounds=3 run, BOTH fleet-synthesis.json.rounds and fleet-report.json.rounds === 2 (the real executed count, NOT 3).
AC3: When no blackboard is configured, fleet-report.json has NO rounds key (byte-identical to today) and no fleet-synthesis.json is written.
AC4: executeRounds returns { executions, roundsRun }; runFleet threads roundsRun into reporter.build (only when a blackboard is used) and collect.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The no-blackboard fleet path stays byte-identical (report shape unchanged, no synthesis file).
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: A no-blackboard runFleet test asserts the report has no rounds key and no fleet-synthesis.json; build(outcomes) with no opts omits rounds; existing fleet tests pass unchanged.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: collect remains pure (no LLM/network); the never-throw exit-0 fleet contract is preserved.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: synthesis.ts has no provider/network import; per-child failure tests still pass; full suite green minus the 6 known cockpit failures.

techNotes.dataModel: PortfolioReport gains optional rounds?: number. SynthesisBundle.rounds value becomes the real executed count.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Mirror Phase B's guarded-spread for optional fields (e.g. the critique flag / optional config sections) to keep absent-key output byte-identical. executeRounds object-return mirrors the codebase's object-spec style (e.g. runner.run({cwd, task})).

metadata.riskNotes: Small, confined change in src/fleet/ (4 source files + 2 test files). Only risk is the executeRounds return-shape change rippling into the Sprint-3 coordinator tests — handled by updating those call sites to destructure .executions.
